rollout_controls.summary: Keep POST /v1/embeddings behind the explicit compat_embeddings_api access-control feature until workflow regression and release smoke stay green.
Keep /v1/tools/invoke on the guarded refusal path unless the dedicated approval-bound execution surface is shipped.
Use scoped compat API tokens and keep compat.models.read, compat.embeddings.create, and compat.tools.invoke permissions explicitly opt-in.
Require typed plugin contracts to negotiate accepted adapters while leaving legacy plugins on the compatibility path.
Treat /console/v1/system/insights, diagnostics observability payloads, and palyra system insights as read-only rollout signals for degraded hotspots.

migration_tracks.surface: palyra config migrate + palyra config recover + console reload planner
migration_tracks.apply: Run config migration before enabling updated compat/provider/retrieval settings, then verify the reload plan or apply result stays explainable and redacted.
migration_tracks.rollback: Use the latest config backup or config recover surface and confirm secrets remain redacted before reloading again.

migration_tracks.surface: bindings schema + typed plugin contract installation
migration_tracks.apply: Reinstall typed plugins with explicit grants and accepted contract adapters before switching operators onto the typed extension path.
migration_tracks.rollback: Remove the typed binding, return to the prior compatibility binding, and rerun plugin explain or doctor to confirm the legacy path is healthy.

migration_tracks.apply: Confirm the registry-backed chat and embeddings models are configured before turning on compat token permissions for embeddings clients.
migration_tracks.rollback: Disable compat embeddings exposure, keep compat models read-only, and fall back to the existing provider status and explain surfaces until provider health is restored.

known_limitations.summary: Compat /v1/tools/invoke intentionally stays on a guarded refusal path; widening it would require a separate approval-bound execution surface.
Typed plugin contracts are additive; untyped legacy plugins remain installable and must keep a visible remediation path instead of hard failure.
Fast workflow regression validates the per-push profile and points at pending full-profile evidence; full regression must still pass before wider release widening.

evidence.summary: Per-push regression run must cover all Phase 8 subsystems and pass the fast scenario set.
Nightly or pre-release regression run must pass the broader runtime matrix before rollout widening.
Critical attack scenarios must remain wired into CI as a release gate.
Performance smoke must continue validating the desktop snapshot and support-bundle hot path before wider rollout.
Support bundle lifecycle validation must remain available for operator troubleshooting evidence.
Release smoke must emit machine-readable package and provenance evidence.
CLI install smoke must continue exporting machine-readable inventory and summary outputs for package validation.
